titleStmt.title: SII 19.303: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 303. (A.R. No. 56 of 1918.) ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE SAME maṇḍapa.

summary.p: This inscription is obscure. It records a gift of 45 sheep for ‘half-a lamp’ to be burnt before the image of Sūryadēva in the temple at Tirumudukuṉṟam by Kāvidikkuḍi Kulamāṉaṉ Kūttaṉ of Neṟkuppai in Paruvūrkūṟṟam a subdivision of Iruṅgōḷappāḍi; also a gift of land by the same donor for the daily supply of an uḻakku of ghee to the temple and some other articles such as a copper-pot, aṇḍōlagattaṇḍu (?). piḍāy and kaḍigai (vessels ?). The mention of Sūryadēva as a principal deity in a shrine is noteworthy. The sense of lines 5 and 6 is not clear.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . kopparakecaripanmaṟkku yāṇṭu 10 2Āvatu Iruṅkoḷappāṭip paruvūrkkū
ṟṟattu neṟkkuppaiyuṭaiyāṉ kāvitikkuṭik kulamāṉaṉ kūttan tirumutukuṉṟattu sūryya
devaṟkku vaitta viḷakku Arai viḷakku Āṭu 4 10 5 candrādittavar panmāheśvararakṣai .
Ivaṉe kulamāṉaṉ kuttaṉ vaitta Aṉtoḷakattaṇṭum piṭāyum kaṭikaiyūm
Itu koṭu poyeṟiṉār niccam Uḻakku ney yaṭṭuvatākavum ceppu kalacappāṉai
niṟai Aimpatiṉm palam Aḻivu col puṉcey Orumā nilam candrādittavaṟ .
Digital edition of SII 19.303 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
